commands.review_summary:
The branch adds four interpreter/search-path env names to the shared host-env denylist, regenerates the macOS policy mirror, and updates docs plus focused sanitizer coverage.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +44, Docs 0, Generated +8. Total +60 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, but not from a live runtime run: current main’s shared policy does not list the four proposed keys, and the sanitizer is entirely policy-driven for inherited and override filtering.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Protected-label, security-boundary, compatibility, and missing-real-proof gates require maintainer and contributor follow-up rather than an automated repair PR.

target.title: fix: expand unsafe host env denylist
target.body:
## Summary

- Expands the shared host environment safety policy to reject additional interpreter startup and search-path variables before they reach host command execution.
- Regenerates the macOS host environment policy from the shared JSON source so app and Node-host sanitizers stay aligned.
- Updates focused regression coverage and user-facing docs that enumerate the blocked environment override classes.
- Intentionally does not change the sanitizer control flow; this keeps the existing policy-driven behavior and only expands the denylist data.

AI-assisted: yes.

## Linked context

No public issue linked in this PR body.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Host environment overrides for additional interpreter startup/search-path variables are rejected by the existing sanitizer path.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux using the repository Vitest wrapper and generated-policy checker.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.policy-parity.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.reported-baseline.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: The focused Vitest run reported 3 test files passed and 34 tests passed.
- Observed result after fix: The new variables are treated as dangerous, removed from inherited and override env inputs, reported in blocked diagnostics, and present in the generated macOS policy.
- What was not tested: Full repository CI and a live node-host command execution path were not run locally.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Local proof covers the shared sanitizer and generated policy parity; GitHub CI is expected to provide broader platform validation.
- Before evidence: Existing tests did not cover these variables in the shared policy baseline.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.policy-parity.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.reported-baseline.test.ts`
- `node scripts/generate-host-env-security-policy-swift.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`

Regression coverage was added for dangerous-key classification, inherited sanitization, override sanitization, diagnostics, reported baseline coverage, and generated macOS policy parity.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: Host command execution environment filtering.

Risk mitigation: The change reuses the existing denylist policy mechanism, keeps ordinary credential/proxy/server-specific variables unaffected, and adds focused tests for the expanded policy surface.

## Current review state

Next action: Wait for automated CI and maintainer review.

Still waiting on: GitHub CI and any maintainer-requested broader validation.

Bot or reviewer comments addressed: None yet.
target.author: pgondhi987
